etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "self" + "assure" + "-ed" from Old English "self" + Latin "assecurare" + "-ed" (to make oneself secure)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Make oneself secure; having confident certain and self-assured confident with certain presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Make secure → confident certain → contemporary self-assured confident and self-assured certain with confident presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Make secure → confident certain → self-assured confident → certain understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having self-assured confident and self-assured certain; experiencing confident certain and self-assured self-assurance with self-assured presence
contemporary_usage.register: Positive; indicates confidence and certain assurance with secure self-assurance
